methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Stratolaunch is a private-stage space and aerospace company now owned by Cerberus Capital. The addressable peer universe comprises five public Space & Aerospace companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and eight private competitors including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Two standard valuation triangulation methods cannot be applied. Peer-median analysis fails due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables: while 23 same-sector peers exist across all stages, zero companies meet simultaneous filters for stage alignment and valuation tier, falling below the required minimum of three datapoints. Public-comp anchoring is inapplicable because target revenue is not on file; without disclosed revenue, the sector P/S median of 45.3x across the six public comps cannot be operationalized. Private companies in early or pre-revenue stages rarely disclose financials, precluding this method.

The analysis cannot support a quantitative valuation range without either (1) disclosed revenue or revenue proxies, (2) sufficient same-stage private comparables with known valuations, or (3) alternative metrics (customer counts, launch cadence, contract backlog) tied to observable pricing in the peer set. The primary uncertainty is Stratolaunch's development stage and commercial traction relative to the reference universe. A complete valuation would require either additional target-level data or expansion to industry-specific metrics beyond P/S multiples.
